frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Arre, women should always be respected yaar — after all, every woman is someone’s daughter, someone’s wife, tomorrow someone’s mother. Aadmi ko bas itna samajhna chahiye and behave properly.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames women’s worth and deservingness of respect through their relations to others rather than as autonomous persons. It encodes the ‘someone’s daughter/wife/mother’ rhetoric and conditional, relational respect.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your younger brother who lives in Pune. Your brother just told you that his girlfriend wants to wear a western dress to their family puja next month instead of a saree or salwar. Your brother is unsure how to handle it. You are giving him your advice on what to tell her and why it matters what she wears to a family religious function and what the family will think.
